steps.title: Spot the key signal
steps.content: The program "identified sidewalks in the vast majority of cases" and even distinguished cobblestone from granite. Hosseini calls this an "already-strong performance." The blank describes how it accomplished those identifications — the word must convey consistent, dependable success.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean consistently and dependably. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — rarely: means infrequently — contradicts "the vast majority of cases," which is the opposite of rarely. ✗
  • Choice B — reliably: means consistently and dependably — matches "vast majority of cases" and the label "already-strong performance." ✓
  • Choice C — slowly: means at low speed — the passage says nothing about the program’s speed; this introduces an unrelated idea. ✗
  • Choice D — clumsily: means in an awkward or inefficient way — directly contradicts the idea of strong performance. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
